user_query:
I'm drafting a paper on how our towns referenced major events across decades, and I need a quick local prototype to extract when a town and a year appear together in the same sentence from some sample clippings. Please build a small, reproducible prototype that:

1) Reads all text files under input/docs/ and the canonical place list at input/places.csv, then extracts one record for every sentence where a place from the list and a four-digit year (1800–1999) co-occur in that sentence. Treat place matching as case-insensitive against the canonical list (match on the exact listed place strings; ignore punctuation). Identify the document by its filename without extension.

2) Writes the structured extraction to output/mentions.jsonl with one JSON object per record including these fields: doc_id (string), place_mention (the surface text matched), place_normalized (exact string from places.csv), year (integer), and sentence_text (the full sentence where the co-mention occurs).

3) Aggregates counts into output/summary.csv with columns: place_normalized, year, count (number of extracted records for that pair across all docs).

4) Validates the extractor by comparing output/mentions.jsonl against the gold annotations in input/gold_mentions.jsonl, considering only the triplet (doc_id, place_normalized, year) for comparison. Run your validation command locally and save its outputs to:
   - output/validation/metrics.json including at least: tp, fp, fn, precision, recall
   - output/validation/diff.csv listing differences with columns: doc_id, place_normalized, year, status ("missing" for gold not found, "spurious" for extracted not in gold)

5) Compose a brief status update at output/status_update.md (no more than one page) that summarizes: what you built, how to run it, how many documents and sentences were processed, total extracted records, and the validation results (tp/fp/fn, precision, recall), plus two concrete next-step improvements.

Inputs are provided under input/. Please do not rely on any external services; keep everything local. Deliver the above outputs under output/ as specified, and ensure the validation step is actually executed to produce the metrics and diff files. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
